summary: World Map & Local Map Feature Testing - 38 tests total (26 backend + 12 frontend). Found and FIXED 1 critical backend bug in create_world_map endpoint. All APIs working correctly.

fixed_in_this_iteration.issue: Line 3572 had `WorldMap().travel_speeds` which failed because WorldMap requires campaign_id and name fields
fixed_in_this_iteration.fix: Changed to use a static default_travel_speeds dictionary instead of instantiating an empty WorldMap model

notes: World Map and Local Maps tabs successfully render and navigate correctly
Upload modals for both World Map and Local Map display with all expected form fields
Sidebar navigation between tabs works correctly

breakdown.test_world_maps.py (backend): 26/26 passed - World Map CRUD, Pin CRUD, Path CRUD, Travel Calculator, Nearby Locations, Local Map CRUD, Local Map Pins, Auth checks
breakdown.world-maps.spec.ts (frontend): 12/12 passed - Tab display, navigation, modal functionality, upload flows

seed_data_creation: Tests create and clean up their own test data using unique identifiers
context_for_next_testing_agent: World Map feature is fully tested and working. Backend API supports: World Map CRUD, pin management (add/update/delete), travel path creation, travel time calculation with different travel modes (walking, horseback, cart, ship, flying), terrain modifiers, and nearby locations. Local Map feature supports: Local Map CRUD linked to campaign locations, and place of interest pins (taverns, shops, temples, etc.). Landing page has the 'Interactive World Maps' feature in the auto-rotating carousel with details about pin cities & landmarks, travel time calculator, and terrain modifiers.

features: World Map tab visible in Campaign Dashboard
Upload World Map modal with form fields
Tab navigation between World Map and other tabs
Local Maps tab visible in Campaign Dashboard
Locations sidebar with All Maps option
Upload Local Map modal with location selector and map type dropdown

rca_of_issue.issue: World Map creation endpoint returning 500 Internal Server Error
rca_of_issue.root_cause: In /app/backend/server.py line 3572, the code attempted to use `WorldMap().travel_speeds` to get default travel speeds. However, WorldMap is a Pydantic model that requires `campaign_id` and `name` fields. Instantiating it without these required fields raised a ValidationError.
rca_of_issue.reproduction: 1. Call POST /api/campaigns/{id}/world-maps with valid map data but without custom travel_speeds. 2. Backend tries to use `WorldMap().travel_speeds` as fallback. 3. Pydantic raises ValidationError due to missing required fields.
rca_of_issue.mitigation: Replaced `WorldMap().travel_speeds` with a static dictionary `default_travel_speeds` containing the same default values (walking: 24, horseback: 48, cart: 16, ship: 72, flying: 96 miles/day).
